flags: our damage is 4/5 physical — one enemy armor item blunts most of it

notes: Their main damage was Argus (30k magical); 2/5 of us itemized magic resist.
Enemy ran 2/5 on or near their meta core.

draftNote: 2 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (support→offlane), Cuban Noobster (midlane→carry).

coaching.headline: A fed Skylar and constant Fangtooth control won it, even with offlane and support read as theirs.
coaching.team: Mid and jungle were favored and we leaned all the way into objective tempo — 24 objective kills to 17, towers 11 to 6. The won Fangtooth fights stacked up: 11.2 (3-1), 17.3 (3-0), and the close-out at our Primal Fangtooth at 28.4 (4-1) that ended it. Skylar went 13/2 and was the engine. The two losses to learn from were the bad trades: the 7.9 open-map loss (0-2) started while our support was pinned in a losing lane, and the 21.3 river trade (0-2) with no major up. We also dropped the 22.6 Primal Fangtooth contest (3-4) — close, but we bounced back by re-grouping for the 28.4 ace instead of forcing it again. That's the model: when a contest goes bad, reset and take the next one with numbers.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Winning the Fangtooth fights at 11.2, 17.3 and the Primal Fangtooth ace at 28.4 (4-1) behind a fed Skylar (13/2) — objective control beat the unfavored offlane and support.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Phase was excellent — 15 assists, 2 deaths, 12 wards (above Mr_Meat31's norm), and Mr_Meat31 enabled the won Fangtooth fights. The early 8.5 skirmish with Skylar (2-0) set tempo. Nothing to fix; this is the support game that lets a fed carry take over.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Wukong was the unfavored offlane (theirs all game vs Gadget) and it showed — 6k hero damage, 193/min, well under Goldilocks324's norm, and no magic resist into a 3-magical comp. The team carried this one, which is fine in a win, but a resist item and more presence in the won fights would have made Wukong a real fourth body instead of a passenger.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: The Fey led the favored mid (10 kills, 27k damage) and was in nearly every winning fight — 11.2, 17.3, 28.4. The 9 deaths are the watch-out: Cuban Noobster trades aggressively, and the 22.6 Primal Fangtooth contest is where that cost came due. Keep the aggression but don't be the one who dies first when a major is on the line.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Carry of the game — Skylar 13/2/8, and the spikes (Viper 12, Vanquisher 25, Plasma Blade 30) meant Willy Guggenheim was online for every Fangtooth fight. Willy closed the 28.4 ace. This is exactly the protected-carry pattern that wins; keep farming safe and letting the frontline open the fights.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Khaimera was the favored jungle and Xeebs was the objective engine — 34k damage to objectives, and present in the won Fangtooth fights (11.2, 28.4). 8 deaths is the only blemish; some came from the 12.6 Mini Prime loss and the 22.6 contest. Pick the dives — Xeebs doesn't need to be in every fight, just the ones around the majors the team is taking.

threats: Chain-CC risk (1 hard-CC abilities, ~1.0s of lockdown): Gadget Security Gate (root 1s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Dive threat on our backline: Yurei. Assign peel for the carry and ward the flanks.
synergy: Little hard CC on our side — we can't force a pick. Play for poke/objectives and punish their cooldowns, don't flip coin-toss 5v5s.
We have sustain (Phase) — favor the longer fight where our heals out-grind them.
Our damage is 4/5 physical — predictable; the enemy itemizes one armor item against most of it. A magical pick would split their defenses.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Telekinetic Link an ally → Psychic Flare blind → Energy Lance → Hyperflux buff ult
ourKits.winCondition: buff a hyper-carry through the Link and Hyperflux for a teamfight
ourKits.passive: abilities heal her and her linked ally
ourKits.playWith: the strongest carry to Link and supercharge
ourKits.playAgainst: kill the linked carry, not Phase; break the link timing

ourKits.combo: Windsurge engage → Cyclebreaker damage-reduction spin → clones from 84000 Hairs
ourKits.winCondition: stance-dance for damage or tankiness and overwhelm with clones
ourKits.playWith: a team that fights alongside his clones
ourKits.playAgainst: AoE clears his clones; CC him during the damage-reduction window

ourKits.combo: Bramble Patch slow → Harvest Nettles stacks → Untamed Growth DoT → Fly Trap pull ult
ourKits.winCondition: Fly Trap a clumped team and detonate AoE on the pull
ourKits.passive: takedowns spawn growths + grant move speed
ourKits.playWith: engage that clumps for her Fly Trap
ourKits.playAgainst: spread out vs the pull; she's squishy — dive her

ourKits.combo: Plasma Grenade → Hypercharge on-hit → Air Assault reposition → Atomiser ult
ourKits.winCondition: Hypercharge and kite-shoot from Air Assault safety
ourKits.passive: extra jump; kills cause corpse explosions
ourKits.playWith: a frontline so she can free-fire airborne
ourKits.playAgainst: CC her out of Air Assault; she's squishy on the ground

ourKits.combo: Ambush leap → Unleash AS → Cull stun, Invigorate to cleanse+heal
ourKits.winCondition: stick on one target, stun with Cull and out-sustain via Spirit Regen
ourKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his Cull stun
ourKits.playAgainst: ANTI-HEAL is mandatory — it guts his whole kit; kite the leap and CC through Invigorate

enemyKits.combo: Security Gate root → Sticky Mine → Tesla Dome zone ult
enemyKits.winCondition: root with the gate and trap a fight inside the Tesla Dome
enemyKits.passive: FDA stacks grant scaling magical power
enemyKits.playWith: frontline that holds enemies in her Dome
enemyKits.playAgainst: dodge the gate; spread vs the Dome — she's immobile, dive her

enemyKits.combo: stance-swap with Zero Shift → Tachyon Lance (ranged) or melee chain → Limit Break on Overheat
enemyKits.winCondition: build Heat and burst with Limit Break, flexing range and melee
enemyKits.passive: damage builds Heat → Overheat unlocks Limit Break
enemyKits.playWith: peel that lets her ramp Heat safely
enemyKits.playAgainst: pressure her before Overheat; CC during the melee stance

enemyKits.combo: Dread Nova stun → Ether Crystal push/lift → Particle Shredder channel → Synaptic Obliterator beams
enemyKits.winCondition: poke from max range and zone with the crystal
enemyKits.playWith: a frontline to protect his stationary channel
enemyKits.playAgainst: dodge Dread Nova; dive him during the immobile ult channel

enemyKits.combo: Bull Rush knockback → Force Shield wall → Shield Slam knockup+stun ult, Shield Bash %hp
enemyKits.winCondition: peel the carry with walls/knockups or engage with Shield Slam
enemyKits.playWith: a carry he can wall for and peel
enemyKits.playAgainst: his Force Shield blocks projectiles — reposition around it; bait Shield Slam

enemyKits.combo: Anima Burst / Whispering Echoes apply Soul Rip → Severance resets on Soul-Ripped targets → Soulstorm pulls them in
enemyKits.winCondition: chain Severance resets to stick to a target and burst in melee
enemyKits.passive: casting grants ranged empowered basics + attack speed
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up CC to hold the target she dashes onto
enemyKits.playAgainst: her chain needs Soul Rip resets — peel/disengage breaks it; punish when Severance is on cooldown

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over timeline majors (older film — no tower times; Fang/Prime/Orb only); itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
